inline_comments.message: Is there another test already implemented that validates the vm-type (I see we have a test for network-role)?   The only concern I have on this one is that the test simply skips the port if it's unable to parse out the vm-type  (see utils.ports.get_invalid_ip_addresses).  I think that's OK, if there's something else to flag an error, but if not it would appear to silently ignore it.

If i'm reading the code correctly, the only way it can validate the parameter is if the vm-type and network-role can be parsed from the resource.  If not, then the parameter check is just skipped.  Should we change this to flag an error rather than silently ignore it?

the test_port_resource_ids.py should be validating that the port resource has vm_type and network_role. You are correct that this test will fall through if unable to parse the vm_type or network_role, however the other test will fail until the port resource id has been corrected.
That works.  I agree - just didn't see the test for vm_type.
